evidence_text: GitHub introduced Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI in experimental mode.
source_excerpt: "Today, in GitHub Copilot CLI, we’re introducing Rubber Duck in experimental mode."
note:
Product availability is explicitly described as experimental, not general availability.
```
caveat:
Product availability is explicitly described as experimental, not general availability.
```

evidence_text: Rubber Duck uses a second model from a different AI family to independently review the primary agent’s plans and work.
source_excerpt: "Rubber Duck leverages a second model from a different AI family to act as an independent reviewer, assessing the agent’s plans and work at the moments where feedback matters most."
note:
This is a product design statement describing how the review step is structured.
```
caveat:
This is a product design statement describing how the review step is structured.
```

evidence_text: When the orchestrator is a Claude model, Rubber Duck is GPT-5.4.
source_excerpt: "When you’ve selected a Claude model from the model picker to use as your orchestrator, Rubber Duck will be GPT-5.4."
note:
The source also says GitHub is exploring other model-family pairings.
```
caveat:
The source also says GitHub is exploring other model-family pairings.
```

evidence_text: In GitHub’s SWE-Bench Pro evaluation, Claude Sonnet 4.6 plus Rubber Duck running GPT-5.4 closed 74.7% of the performance gap between Sonnet and Opus.
source_excerpt: "Claude Sonnet 4.6 paired with Rubber Duck running GPT-5.4 achieved a resolution rate approaching Claude Opus 4.6 running alone, closing 74.7% of the performance gap between Sonnet and Opus."
note:
This is a vendor-reported evaluation result from the blog post rather than an independently reproduced result in the captured source.
```
caveat:
This is a vendor-reported evaluation result from the blog post rather than an independently reproduced result in the captured source.
```

evidence_text: GitHub says Rubber Duck tends to help more on difficult problems spanning 3 or more files and normally taking 70 or more steps.
source_excerpt: "We noticed that Rubber Duck tends to help more with difficult problems, ones that span 3+ files and would normally take 70+ steps."
note:
This is a scoped tendency claim tied to GitHub’s evaluation observations, not a universal performance law.
```
caveat:
This is a scoped tendency claim tied to GitHub’s evaluation observations, not a universal performance law.
```

evidence_text: On problems spanning 3 or more files and 70 or more steps, Sonnet plus Rubber Duck scored 3.8% higher than the Sonnet baseline.
source_excerpt: "On these problems, Sonnet + Rubber Duck scores 3.8% higher than the Sonnet baseline"
note:
The source does not provide the absolute baseline score in the captured text.
```
caveat:
The source does not provide the absolute baseline score in the captured text.
```

evidence_text: On the hardest problems identified across three trials, Sonnet plus Rubber Duck scored 4.8% higher than the Sonnet baseline.
source_excerpt: "and 4.8% higher on the hardest problems identified across three trials."
note:
The captured source does not define the full selection method for the "hardest problems" beyond this phrase.
```
caveat:
The captured source does not define the full selection method for the "hardest problems" beyond this phrase.
```

evidence_text: GitHub Copilot may automatically seek a Rubber Duck critique after drafting a plan.
source_excerpt: "For complex work, GitHub Copilot may seek a critique automatically at the checkpoints where feedback has the highest return: After drafting a plan"
note:
The source frames this as an automatic checkpoint for complex work, not a guaranteed invocation on every task.
```
caveat:
The source frames this as an automatic checkpoint for complex work, not a guaranteed invocation on every task.
```

evidence_text: GitHub Copilot may automatically seek a Rubber Duck critique after a complex implementation.
source_excerpt: "For complex work, GitHub Copilot may seek a critique automatically at the checkpoints where feedback has the highest return: ... After a complex implementation"
note:
The source presents this as one of several automatic checkpoint types.
```
caveat:
The source presents this as one of several automatic checkpoint types.
```

evidence_text: GitHub Copilot may automatically seek a Rubber Duck critique after writing tests and before executing them.
source_excerpt: "For complex work, GitHub Copilot may seek a critique automatically at the checkpoints where feedback has the highest return: ... After writing tests, before executing them"
note:
The stated purpose is to catch test-coverage gaps or flawed assertions before test execution reinforces a false pass condition.
```
caveat:
The stated purpose is to catch test-coverage gaps or flawed assertions before test execution reinforces a false pass condition.
```

evidence_text: The agent may seek a Rubber Duck critique reactively when it is stuck in a loop or cannot make progress.
source_excerpt: "The agent can also seek a critique reactively if it gets stuck in a loop or can’t make progress."
note:
This describes a reactive trigger condition separate from the automatic checkpoints for complex work.
```
caveat:
This describes a reactive trigger condition separate from the automatic checkpoints for complex work.
```

evidence_text: Users can request a Rubber Duck critique at any point.
source_excerpt: "As a user, you can request a critique at any point."
note:
The source distinguishes this from automatic and reactive invocations initiated by Copilot itself.
```
caveat:
The source distinguishes this from automatic and reactive invocations initiated by Copilot itself.
```

evidence_text: Rubber Duck is invoked through Copilot’s existing task tool infrastructure.
source_excerpt: "For the technically curious: Rubber Duck is invoked through Copilot’s existing task tool—the same infrastructure used for other subagents."
note: This is the most concrete implementation detail in the captured source about how the review agent is wired into Copilot CLI.
caveat: This is the most concrete implementation detail in the captured source about how the review agent is wired into Copilot CLI.
